entities.name: CHICKPEA
entities.description: Cicer arietinum L., the crop studied for its response to salinity stress.

entities.name: SOIL SALINITY
entities.description: The presence of excess salts in the soil which acts as an environmental stressor.

entities.name: VEGETATIVE PHASE
entities.description: The growth stage of the plant prior to the initiation of reproductive organs.

entities.name: REPRODUCTIVE PHASE
entities.description: The growth stage of the plant involving flowering and pod development.

entities.name: ROOT-ZONE SALINITY TREATMENTS
entities.description: The application of varying concentrations of NaCl in aerated nutrient solution to plant roots.

entities.name: NACL
entities.description: Sodium chloride used to induce salinity stress in the experimental setup.

entities.name: JG11
entities.description: A chickpea cultivar previously considered tolerant to salinity.

entities.name: SALT SENSITIVITY
entities.description: The susceptibility of the plant to the negative effects of salinity.

entities.name: DRY MASS
entities.description: The weight of the plant material after water removal, used to measure growth.

entities.name: FLOWER NUMBERS
entities.description: The quantity of flowers produced by the plant.

entities.name: POD FORMATION
entities.description: The process of converting flowers into pods, identified as a highly sensitive stage.

entities.name: POLLEN GERMINATION
entities.description: The process of pollen grains sprouting, which is inhibited by NaCl.

entities.name: TUBE GROWTH
entities.description: The elongation of the pollen tube, which is inhibited by NaCl.

entities.name: SALT TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of the plant to maintain growth and productivity under saline conditions.

entities.name: SHOOT CL
entities.description: Chlorine ions accumulated in the shoot of the plant.

entities.name: SHOOT NA
entities.description: Sodium ions accumulated in the shoot of the plant.
